intro: Phanera audax, also known as the bold Phanera, is a species of climbing plant in the family Caesalpiniaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Phanera audax contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, as well as being incorporated into remedies for coughs, fever, and other respiratory complaints.
